what_it_does: It is a durable, human-readable ledger of test suites that are RED on main but whose fix belongs to a specific work lane; each entry records what is red, why, who owns it, the recommended fix, and its open/cleared status, acting as a cross-fork fix-board.
observations: The file splits entries into OPEN and CLEARED sections and tracks status, owner, diagnosis, and recommended fix.
It distinguishes cross-lane ordering reds from code bugs and explicitly assigns fixes to the owning lane.
It references concrete commits, dates, files, line numbers, and other documents such as STATE.md and design/_system/addresses.json.
It repeatedly notes that a red in one lane is NOT another lane's breach and should not be edited by the wrong owner.
It differentiates the full all-green convergence-round gate from a lower per-criterion commit bar.
Entries include cast_beyond_listening_acceptance, ui_registry_acceptance, drift_acceptance, and wire_trigger_acceptance.
standouts: It treats main redness as a cross-fork coordination artifact rather than only a quality failure.
It refuses to let one lane edit another lane's tests or registry, enforcing ownership boundaries.
It records both cleared and still-open reds with verification details, making session-to-session state visible.
conventions: Markdown header hierarchy with OPEN and CLEARED sections.
Structured bullet fields: Found, failing check, why, significance, recommended fix, blocks, status.
Neutral, non-accusatory phrasing such as 'NOT a cognition breach'.
Use of commit hashes, dates, and exact source locations for traceability.
Cross-references to other project documents and suites.
concerns: The same suite can appear in both OPEN and CLEARED with different statuses, which may confuse readers scanning one section only.
The CLEARED section contains a currently RED drift entry for generate_mockup_acceptance, blurring the open/cleared boundary.
Heavy lane-specific jargon makes the file hard to parse without prior context.
notes: This is the project's cross-fork red-team board: it writes down who must fix which standing red and why, so nothing gets lost between sessions.
questions: What is the exact protocol for moving an entry from OPEN to CLEARED?
How do the readiness gate and convergence round mechanically interact with this board?
How and when will this ledger fold into the finding store Group C?
What is the precise definition of the per-criterion commit bar versus the full all-green gate?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is a durable, owner-assigned record of standing reds and their fix status.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between parallel work lanes
Distinguishing dependency/ordering reds from real defects
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent silent-red-across-sessions failures by making ownership, diagnosis, and the required fix explicit for every standing red on main.
novelty: It deliberately frames CI redness as a coordination problem across lanes and protects each lane's ownership of its own fixes and tests.
contribution: It contributes a durable, owner-assigned, human-readable record of standing red suites and their resolution status that a fused system could keep for cross-session coordination.
summary_for_embedding: OPEN-REDS.md is a ledger of test suites that are red on main with lane-assigned ownership, diagnosis, recommended fixes, and open/cleared status, serving as a cross-fork fix-board.
intention_for_embedding: It was created to prevent silent-red-across-sessions failures by tracking which lane must fix each standing red before the convergence-round all-green gate.
